brief.topic: HH the Amir receives Pakistani Prime Minister at Amiri Diwan
brief.entity: HH Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani
brief.facts: HH the Amir received HE Mr Shehbaz Sharif at the Amiri Diwan on Saturday. The talks reviewed bilateral relations and ways to expand cooperation. Regional developments in the Middle East were also discussed.
brief.quote: We value Pakistan's stance and look forward to deeper economic cooperation between our two countries. — HH the Amir

brief.topic: Mathaf opens 'Resolutions' exhibition marking 15 years of the museum
brief.entity: Mathaf: Arab Museum of Modern Art
brief.facts: Mathaf: Arab Museum of Modern Art has opened a new exhibition titled 'Resolutions' to mark 15 years since the museum's establishment, Qatar Museums announced. The exhibition runs from 10 June and is on display at the museum in Education City. According to the museum, 'Resolutions' examines Mathaf's foundation through four defining areas: its origins under Sheikh Hassan bin Mohammed bin Ali Al Thani, its exhibition history since opening in 2010, its engagement with knowledge circulation in the Arab world, and its educational programmes. The museum said the show draws on works from its permanent collection. Entry is free for all visitors, and Qatar Museums said accompanying public talks and guided tours will be held during the exhibition period.

brief.topic: Ministry of Public Health launches summer mosquito-control programme
brief.facts: MoPH is deploying drones and biological fish-based larvicide across 18 districts to control mosquito populations during summer. The programme is coordinated with the Ministry of Municipality and runs through October.
brief.quote: Public-health vigilance during summer is a shared responsibility. — MoPH Director of Communicable Diseases

brief.topic: Qatar Education Leadership Expo 2026 concludes in Doha
brief.facts: The Qatar Education Leadership Expo 2026 concluded in Doha on 24 May, organisers said. The event brought together school leaders, educators and education-sector officials over its run. Organisers said the programme featured sessions on school leadership, digital learning and student wellbeing. The expo has now ended and is no longer accepting registrations. Editorial note: the listing remained on the live events page after the closing date and is being updated to reflect that the event has concluded.

brief.topic: Qatar Airways resumes direct flights to Helsinki and Tokyo Haneda
brief.facts: Qatar Airways will resume flights to Helsinki, Finland, and Tokyo Haneda, Japan, from 15 July, the airline announced. The Helsinki route will operate four times weekly, increasing to daily from 1 August. Tokyo Haneda will receive four weekly flights starting 15 July, complementing the existing Tokyo Narita service. The expansion brings Qatar Airways' global network to over 160 destinations this summer.
brief.quote: We are pleased to restore connectivity with Helsinki and Tokyo Haneda — two strategically important markets for our network. — Qatar Airways Chief Commercial Officer

rewrite_samples.label: Casual event hype → institutional
rewrite_samples.raw: Qatar is launching a huge new event and it's going to be amazing. Lots of people are expected and this could be one of the biggest things this year. You won't believe how cool the line-up is.

rewrite_samples.label: Marketing press release → news brief
rewrite_samples.raw: We are SUPER excited to announce our game-changing new platform that will revolutionize how Qatar shops online! This unprecedented experience is mind-blowing and absolutely must-see for every consumer in the country!!!

rewrite_samples.label: Casual social caption → news lead
rewrite_samples.raw: Crazy news today — Qatar Airways is back in Helsinki and Tokyo. Insane move from the airline. Honestly obsessed with how fast they're expanding everywhere lol.

sample_dirty_body:
Qatar Airways has announced the resumption of flights to Helsinki and Tokyo Haneda starting 15 July. The airline confirmed the new schedule will operate four times weekly between Doha and Helsinki, and will be complemented by a daily service to Tokyo Haneda.  Officials highlighted that the move expands Qatar Airways' global network to over 160 destinations.

The expansion forms part of the carrier's summer 2026 commercial plan, the airline said in a statement. Make sure to check out our social media to keep track of the latest content! Qatar Living Facebook: facebook.com/qatarliving — Qatar Living Twitter: twitter.com/qatarliving — Qatar Living Instagram: instagram.com/qatarliving
